user_message: Just came out of renewal risk with Pacific Northwest Outfitters. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-10 08:09 EST
Subject: Re: Re: renewal risk Timeline

Just checking in on the renewal risk proposal. Our IT Director is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
Date: 2024-02-19 14:07 EST
Subject: Follow-up: renewal risk Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with renewal risk. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-05 12:17 EST
Subject: renewal risk - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with renewal risk. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Pacific Northwest Outfitters: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-G trap (Misattributes statements across threads); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-G failure mode (Misattributes statements across threads)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-G -- Misattributes statements across threads
